storage_policy.copyright_boundary: Store only paraphrased design moves, derived rules, and code-generation obligations; do not store copied media, transcripts, source layouts, source brand, or raw assets.

units.tutorial_anchor: Visual storytelling guidance reduced to a generator rule: keep only the data that changes the audience decision.
units.observed_design_move: The decisive claim is isolated first, then secondary evidence is delayed or routed away from the main canvas.
units.derived_rule: Each slide must declare the one decision-driving object before any proof detail is drawn.
units.code_generation_binding: Bind the slide to a native PPT deletion and routing pass before rendering text boxes or proof shapes.
units.native_ppt_obligation: Use editable text and native shapes for the retained claim, with routed detail represented as trace data rather than visible clutter.
units.failure_probe: Fails if the slide reads as a report page with equal-weight evidence blocks.
units.anti_copy_boundary: Do not copy source visuals, source brand, transcripts, audio, lesson prose, or source layouts.
units.qa_probe: Contact sheet review should show one dominant decision object before supporting evidence is legible.

units.tutorial_anchor: Type hierarchy guidance converted into explicit native text roles, minimum sizes, and readable line lengths.
units.observed_design_move: Headline, proof phrase, label, and note are separated by scale and placement instead of color alone.
units.derived_rule: Before generation, assign each text object to a type role with a minimum size and a maximum line length.
units.code_generation_binding: Bind type roles to native PPT fontSize values, line limits, and zero letter spacing before slide layout.
units.native_ppt_obligation: Create editable native text boxes with explicit type roles and no negative tracking.
units.failure_probe: Fails if title, body, labels, and notes collapse into equal visual weight.
units.anti_copy_boundary: Do not copy tutorial examples, source brand, diagrams, screenshots, prose, or type specimens.
units.qa_probe: Contact sheet review should identify the reading order before small text is inspected.

units.tutorial_anchor: Slide makeover guidance reduced to a rule that long prose becomes a small number of native visual steps.
units.observed_design_move: A dense paragraph is split into a before state, an action, and an after state with fewer labels.
units.derived_rule: Convert any long block into two to four native step objects with one visible delta per step.
units.code_generation_binding: Bind text reduction to native PPT beforeAfter and step modules before adding explanatory copy.
units.native_ppt_obligation: Represent steps with editable shapes, connectors, and labels rather than rasterized text or copied examples.
units.failure_probe: Fails if the slide keeps the original prose as a large block.
units.anti_copy_boundary: Do not copy makeover examples, source layouts, source brand, exercise material, or course prose.
units.qa_probe: Contact sheet review should show a visible before to after path with reduced wording.

units.tutorial_anchor: Design-first generation guidance converted into an explicit ordering rule: select memory, then gate, then code.
units.observed_design_move: The design decision is named before implementation, so code renders a committed layout instead of improvising.
units.derived_rule: Each slide role must receive selected decomposition units, executable memory bindings, and gate checks before native PPT code runs.
units.code_generation_binding: Bind selected design memory to native PPT function names and parameters before any slide module is called.
units.native_ppt_obligation: Generate only editable native objects whose trace records the selected data, memory, and workflow gate.
units.failure_probe: Fails if generated code invents layout choices without traceable memory bindings.
units.anti_copy_boundary: Do not copy product surfaces, source UI, source brand, icons, screenshots, or interaction layouts.
units.qa_probe: Trace review should show that design selections happened before native PPT generation.

units.tutorial_anchor: Video and audio rhythm guidance reduced to a native climax object with a deliberate pause and minimal labels.
units.observed_design_move: The strongest proof object expands while explanation waits until the focal object is understood.
units.derived_rule: The climax slide must reserve the largest canvas zone for one native hero object before labels and secondary proof appear.
units.code_generation_binding: Bind the climax to a native PPT heroObject module with a forty four to fifty eight percent main-canvas occupation rule.
units.native_ppt_obligation: Build the hero object, highlight, connector, and label as editable native PPT shapes and text.
units.failure_probe: Fails if the climax is another grid or if the focal object is visually secondary.
units.anti_copy_boundary: Do not copy video frames, demo UI, source camera composition, audio, transcripts, or source brand.
units.qa_probe: Contact sheet review should show the hero object as the first-read element on the climax slide.

units.tutorial_anchor: Case evidence is kept as sanitized source-to-rule memory, not as borrowed brand or layout material.
units.observed_design_move: Commercial evidence supports the workflow claim while brand-specific surface details are removed.
units.derived_rule: Every proof object must use original naming, neutral shapes, and traceable source-boundary checks.
units.code_generation_binding: Bind source sanitization to a native PPT workflowGate check before evidence shapes are rendered.
units.native_ppt_obligation: Use editable native evidence objects with original labels and explicit source-boundary trace fields.
units.failure_probe: Fails if evidence resembles a source product page or branded launch layout.
units.anti_copy_boundary: Do not copy source UI, source brand, product names, icons, screenshots, launch prose, or source layouts.
units.qa_probe: Review should confirm the evidence is commercially specific but visually original.
